This stage is the system’s central request/response loop: after startup has prepared a session, it runs each conversational turn from queued input through model interaction, tool use, streaming UI updates, and final bookkeeping. core/src/tasks/regular.rs is the outer task entrypoint for normal chat turns, emitting the initial turn-start signal, reusing any prewarmed session, and rerunning the engine until all pending input is drained. core/src/session/turn.rs is the main orchestrator, deciding whether to compact history first, processing hooks and inputs, assembling tools and skills, invoking the model, handling retries, and committing the completed turn.

Compaction support is split by strategy: core/src/tasks/compact.rs chooses manual compaction mode, core/src/compact.rs performs local summarization and transcript rebuilding, and core/src/compact_remote.rs delegates compaction to provider endpoints and installs sanitized results. core/src/turn_metadata.rs prepares the lineage, sandbox, client, and Git metadata attached to model and MCP requests.

Two sub-stages plug into this loop: Model transport execution sends and streams inference over the supported transports, while Streaming reduction and UI projection turns raw streamed events into stable transcript and terminal state. For code-mode turns, code-mode/src/runtime/mod.rs, module_loader.rs, and timers.rs host the embedded V8 runtime, evaluate user JavaScript, resolve async tool calls, and provide minimal timer support so JS execution can participate in the same turn lifecycle.
